meta.title: Host resistance to Candida albicans in uraemia -- Miller and Findon 17 (2): 181 -- Journal of Medical Microbiology

abstractHtml: In renal failure, infection is a frequent complication and cause of death, which suggests that uraemia impairs immunity. In a comparison of Candida albicans fungaemia, peritonitis and subcutaneous infection in normal and severely uraemic rats, uraemia did not affect the course of these infections. C. albicans is an important pathogen in immunocompromised hosts and we conclude that uraemia is unlikely to be the primary factor that increases host susceptibility to infection with this fungus.
